title: Container-dependent handling of non-JSON values (drop vs throw)

findingDetails.evidence: As an object value, function/symbol/undefined are silently skipped (line 41); as an array element, function/symbol fall through to the line 53 TypeError throw while undefined becomes null (line 33 map -> line 49-51). Same unsupported value either vanishes or crashes depending on container.
findingDetails.why: A 'canonical' fingerprint's failure mode should not depend on whether an unsupported value sits in an object vs an array; it surprises callers and diverges from JSON.stringify (which emits null for array functions, never throws).
findingDetails.recommendation: Handle function/symbol/undefined uniformly across containers — either canonicalize array holes to null AND objects consistently, or throw in both — and document the chosen contract.

title: Non-finite numbers throw uncaught in the memo-planning batch

findingDetails.evidence: normalizeNumber throws on NaN/Infinity (canonical-fingerprint.ts:13-15); planMemoizedIndexing calls canonicalFingerprint(input.canonicalInput) at line 744 with no try/catch, so a single non-finite field aborts the entire batch plan instead of skipping one input.
findingDetails.why: A defensive throw deep in the fingerprinter becomes a whole-batch failure at an unguarded call site; JSON.stringify would have silently produced 'null'. Currently test-only so not live, but the call site is the latent risk.
findingDetails.recommendation: Wrap the per-input canonicalFingerprint call in try/catch and treat a fingerprint failure as a memo miss for that one input, or pre-validate canonicalInput for finiteness.
